creator: makc@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Wed, 29 Jun 2011 01:10:58 +0300, Aldis Berjoza wrote:
> On Tue, 28 Jun 2011 19:51:05 GMT
> makc@FreeBSD.org wrote:
> 
> > Synopsis: [PATCH] print/lyx-devel update
> > 
> > State-Changed-From-To: open->feedback
> > State-Changed-By: makc
> > State-Changed-When: Tue Jun 28 19:51:04 UTC 2011
> > State-Changed-Why: 
> > Is there a reason to have both lyx-1.6 and lyx-2 in ports? I'd update
> > print/lyx to 2.0 and removed lyx-devel.
> 
> 
> Frankly I don't know. I almost haven't been using Lyx. I do my TeXing
> in Vim.
> 
> v1.6 is still supported by upstream, so we could keep it for a while.
> I don't know how stable v2.0 is.

I see 1.6.10 was released after 2.0 and lyx developers recommend to keep 1.6 for a while. Since 1.6.10 is a final in 1.6 series I propose to copy print/lyx to print/lyx16 and update print/lyx to 2.0.

> Current lyx-devel (before this patch) has this problem:
> http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=ports/156034
> 
> It looks like it's fixed in v2.0 release, but I'm not 100% sure. At
> least it looked like it was working fine, when I tested it.
> 
> 
> On the side note: Why do we have print/lyx14 ?

This is the last version based on qt3.
